Off Calendar Feb-25-2011 Denied
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for plaintiff's motion for a protective order.
Dept. 611, Judge: James J. McBride, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Joe Vickstein, CSR #4780.

Cassiana Aaronson, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Robert Ruhl & Beverly Ruhl.
Deborah L. Cote, Esq. appeared for defendant Union Carbide.

After hearing argument by counsel, and good cause appearing, plaintiff's motion for a protective order is DENIED. Plaintiff shall respond to defendant's discovery request pursuant to California law.
Defendant's motion for sanctions is GRANTED. Plaintiff shall pay defendant sanctions in the amount of $1,750.00.

Counsel for defendant shall prepare the Order After Hearing.

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Jan-28-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Christopher R. Nelson, Esq. of VASQUEZ ESTRADA & DUMONT LLP (415-453-1555), specially appearing for defendant / moving party FDCC CALIFORNIA INC.
No other appearances.

RULING:
Defendant FDCC California Inc.'s motion to quash service of plaintiff's summons. No argument was made and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Defendant's motion is granted. Plaintiff's summons fails to specify that FDCC was being served as a Doe. CCP 474 provides that a summons must provide a DOE defendant with notice of the fictitious name under which it was designated in the complaint. In Armstrong v. Superior Court (1956) 144 Cal.App.2d 420, 424 the court held that when a summons fails to identify the fictitious name under which a defendant is being sued, the 'effect is to make the service of such a defective summons insufficient to give the court jurisdiction over the person of the defendant.' (See also Carol Gilbert, Inc. v. Haller 179 Cal.App.4th 852). Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

Off Calendar Oct-27-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: Paul Bird, Atty. for defendant and moving party: Union Carbide Corporation; Rett D. Bergmark, Atty. for Plaintiff and Opposing party: Keith Simmons

RULING: HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Defendant Union Carbide Corporation's Motion To Compel Amended, Verified Responses To Special Interrogatories, Form Interrogatories, And Requests For Production is granted as Plaintiff's objections are overruled and ordered stricken as to the form and special interrogatories. The responses are inadequate. The motion is granted as to the request for documents as the objections are overruled and ordered stricken, except as to privilege. All documents withheld pursuant to a claim of privilege shall be identified in a privilege log. Further responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Order was signed in open court. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. REPORTER: Rita R. Lerner, CSR# 3179
